MAT_3_15.speaker: Jesus
MAT_3_15.normalized: let it be so now it is fitting for us to fulfill all righteousness in this way

anchor.BSB: It is written: 'Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.

translations.NRSVUE: “Do not judge, so that you may not be judged.
translations.DBH: 1 “Judge not, that you may not be judged;
translations.LAMSA: JUDGE not, that you may not be judged.

anchor.BSB: Do not judge, or you will be judged.

MAT_7_2.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_2.normalized: for with the same judgment you pronounce you will be judged and with the measure you use it will be measured to you

translations.NRSVUE: For the judgment you give will be the judgment you get, and the measure you give will be the measure you get.
translations.DBH: 2 for by whatever verdict you pass judgment you shall be judged, and in whatever measure you measure out it shall be meted out to you.
translations.LAMSA: For with the same judgment that you judge, you will be judged, and with the same measure with which you measure, it will be measured to you.

anchor.BSB: For with the same judgment you pronounce, you will be judged, and with the measure you use, it will be measured to you.

MAT_7_3.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_3.normalized: why do you look at the speck in your brother s eye but fail to notice the beam in your own eye

translations.NRSVUE: Why do you see the speck in your neighbor’s eye but do not notice the log in your own eye?
translations.DBH: 3 And why do you look at the straw in your brother’s eye, yet do not perceive the beam in your own eye?
translations.LAMSA: Why do you see the splinter which is in your brother’s eye, and do not feel the beam which is in your own eye?

anchor.BSB: Why do you look at the speck in your brother's eye but fail to notice the beam in your own eye?

MAT_7_4.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_4.normalized: how can you say to your brother let me take the speck out of your eye while there is still a beam in your own eye

translations.NRSVUE: Or how can you say to your neighbor, ‘Let me take the speck out of your eye,’ while the log is in your own eye?
translations.DBH: 4 How is it that you will say to your brother, ‘Let me take that straw out of your eye,’ and look: The beam is in your eye?
translations.LAMSA: Or how can you say to your brother, let me take out the splinter from your eye, and behold there is a cross beam in your own eye?

anchor.BSB: How can you say to your brother, 'Let me take the speck out of your eye,' while there is still a beam in your own eye?

MAT_7_5.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_5.normalized: you hypocrite first take the beam out of your own eye and then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother s eye

translations.NRSVUE: You hypocrite, first take the log out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to take the speck out of your neighbor’s eye.
translations.DBH: 5 Charlatan, first pluck the beam out of your eye, and then you will see clearly how to pluck the straw out of your brother’s eye.
translations.LAMSA: O hypocrites, first take out the beam from your own eye, and then you will see clearly to get out the splinter from your brother’s eye.

anchor.BSB: You hypocrite! First take the beam out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother's eye.

MAT_7_6.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_6.normalized: do not give dogs what is holy do not throw your pearls before swine if you do they may trample them under their feet and then turn and tear you to pieces

translations.NRSVUE: “Do not give what is holy to dogs, and do not throw your pearls before swine, or they will trample them under foot and turn and maul you.
translations.DBH: 6 Do not give what is holy to the dogs, neither cast your pearls before swine, lest they trample them with their feet and, turning, shatter you.
translations.LAMSA: ¶Do not give holy things to the dogs; and do not throw your pearls before the swine, for they might tread them with their feet, and then turn and rend you.

anchor.BSB: Do not give dogs what is holy; do not throw your pearls before swine. If you do, they may trample them under their feet, and then turn and tear you to pieces.

MAT_7_7.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_7.normalized: ask and it will be given to you seek and you will find knock and the door will be opened to you

translations.DBH: 7 Ask, and it shall be given to you; seek, and you shall find; knock, and it shall be opened to you.
translations.LAMSA: Ask, and it shall be given to you; seek, and you shall find; knock and it shall be opened to you.

anchor.BSB: Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and the door will be opened to you.

MAT_7_8.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_8.normalized: for everyone who asks receives he who seeks finds and to him who knocks the door will be opened

translations.NRSVUE: For everyone who asks receives, and everyone who searches finds, and for everyone who knocks, the door will be opened.
translations.DBH: 8 For everyone who asks receives, and everyone who seeks finds, and to everyone who knocks it shall be opened.
translations.LAMSA: For whoever asks, receives; and he who seeks, finds; and to him who knocks, the door is opened.

anchor.BSB: For everyone who asks receives; he who seeks finds; and to him who knocks, the door will be opened.

MAT_7_9.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_9.normalized: which of you if his son asks for bread will give him a stone

translations.LAMSA: Or who is the man among you, who when his son asks him for bread, why, will he hand him a stone?

anchor.BSB: Which of you, if his son asks for bread, will give him a stone?

MAT_7_10.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_10.normalized: or if he asks for a fish will give him a snake

translations.LAMSA: Or if he should ask him for fish, why, will he hand him a snake?

anchor.BSB: Or if he asks for a fish, will give him a snake?

MAT_7_11.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_11.normalized: so if you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children how much more will your father in heaven give good things to those who ask him

translations.NRSVUE: If you, then, who are evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in heaven give good things to those who ask him!
translations.DBH: 11 If you, therefore, who are wicked, know to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in the heavens give good things to those who ask him.
translations.LAMSA: If therefore you who err, know how to give good gifts to your sons, how much more will your Father in heaven give good things to those who ask him?

anchor.BSB: So if you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in heaven give good things to those who ask Him!

MAT_7_12.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_12.normalized: in everything then do to others as you would have them do to you for this is the essence of the law and the prophets

translations.NRSVUE: “In everything do to others as you would have them do to you, for this is the Law and the Prophets.

translations.LAMSA: Whatever you wish men to do for you, do likewise also for them; for this is the law and the prophets.

anchor.BSB: In everything, then, do to others as you would have them do to you. For this is the essence of the Law and the Prophets.

MAT_7_13.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_13.normalized: enter through the narrow gate for wide is the gate and broad is the way that leads to destruction and many enter through it

translations.DBH: 13 “Enter through the narrow gate; for the path leading away to destruction is broad and open, and there are many who enter by it;
translations.LAMSA: Enter in through the narrow door, for wide is the door, and broad is the road which carries to destruction, and many are those who travel on it.

anchor.BSB: Enter through the narrow gate. For wide is the gate and broad is the way that leads to destruction, and many enter through it.

MAT_7_14.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_14.normalized: but small is the gate and narrow the way that leads to life and only a few find it

translations.DBH: 14 for narrow is the gate and close-cramped the path leading away to life, and those who find it are few.
translations.LAMSA: O how narrow is the door, and how difficult is the road which carries to life, and few are those who are found on it.

anchor.BSB: But small is the gate and narrow the way that leads to life, and only a few find it.

MAT_7_15.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_15.normalized: beware of false prophets they come to you in sheep s clothing but inwardly they are ravenous wolves

translations.LAMSA: Be careful of false prophets who come to you in lamb’s clothing, but within they are ravening wolves.

anchor.BSB: Beware of false prophets. They come to you in sheep's clothing, but inwardly they are ravenous wolves.

MAT_7_16.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_16.normalized: by their fruit you will recognize them are grapes gathered from thornbushes or figs from thistles

translations.LAMSA: You will know them by their fruits. Why, do they gather grapes from thorns, or figs from thistles?

anchor.BSB: By their fruit you will recognize them. Are grapes gathered from thornbushes, or figs from thistles?

MAT_7_17.normalized: likewise every good tree bears good fruit but a bad tree bears bad fruit

translations.LAMSA: So every good tree bears good fruits; but a bad tree bears bad fruits.

anchor.BSB: Likewise, every good tree bears good fruit, but a bad tree bears bad fruit.

MAT_7_18.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_18.normalized: a good tree cannot bear bad fruit and a bad tree cannot bear good fruit

translations.LAMSA: good tree cannot bear bad fruits, neither can a bad tree bear good fruits.

anchor.BSB: A good tree cannot bear bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot bear good fruit.

translations.LAMSA: Any tree which does not bear good fruits will be cut down and cast into the fire.

anchor.BSB: Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.

MAT_7_20.normalized: so then by their fruit you will recognize them

translations.LAMSA: Thus by their fruits you will know them.

MAT_7_21.normalized: not everyone who says to me lord lord will enter the kingdom of heaven but only he who does the will of my father in heaven

translations.LAMSA: great many will say to me in that day, My Lord, my Lord, did we not prophesy in your name, and in your name cast out devils, and in your name do many wonders?

translations.NRSVUE: And Jesus said to them, “The wedding attendants cannot mourn as long as the bridegroom is with them, can they? The days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast.

translations.LAMSA: Jesus said to them, Is it possible for those at the wedding feast to fast as long as the bridegroom is with them? But the days are coming, when the bridegroom will be taken from them, and then they will fast.

anchor.BSB: How can the guests of the bridegroom mourn while He is with them? But the time will come when the bridegroom will be taken from them; then they will fast.

MAT_9_16.speaker: Jesus
MAT_9_16.normalized: no one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment for the patch will pull away from the garment and a worse tear will result

translations.NRSVUE: No one sews a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old cloak, for the patch pulls away from the cloak, and a worse tear is made.
translations.DBH: 16 And no one puts a patch of unfulled cloth on an old garment; for the filling tears away from the garment and a worse rent appears.
translations.LAMSA: No man puts a new patch on an old garment, so as not to weaken that garment, and make the hole larger.

anchor.BSB: No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment. For the patch will pull away from the garment, and a worse tear will result.

MAT_9_17.speaker: Jesus
MAT_9_17.normalized: neither do men pour new wine into old wineskins if they do the skins will burst the wine will spill and the wineskins will be ruined instead they pour new wine into new wineskins and both are preserved

translations.NRSVUE: Neither is new wine put into old wineskins; otherwise, the skins burst, and the wine is spilled, and the skins are ruined, but new wine is put into fresh wineskins, and so both are preserved.”
translations.DBH: 17 Neither do they put new wine in old wineskins; for if they should the wineskins are burst open, and the wine is spilled out, and the wineskins are destroyed. Rather, they put new wine into fresh wineskins, and both are together preserved.”
translations.LAMSA: Neither do they pour new wine into worn out skins, so as not to rend the skins, and spill the wine, and the wine runs out, and the skins are ruined; but they pour new wine into new skins, and both of them are well preserved.

anchor.BSB: Neither do men pour new wine into old wineskins. If they do, the skins will burst, the wine will spill, and the wineskins will be ruined. Instead, they pour new wine into new wineskins, and both are preserved.

MAT_9_22.speaker: Jesus
MAT_9_22.normalized: take courage daughter your faith has healed you

translations.NRSVUE: Jesus turned, and seeing her he said, “Take heart, daughter; your faith has made you well.” And the woman was made well from that moment.
translations.DBH: 22 And, turning and seeing her, Jesus said, “Take heart, daughter; your faith has healed you.” And from that hour the woman was healed.
translations.LAMSA: And Jesus turned around and saw her and said to her, Have courage, my daughter, your faith has healed you; and the woman was healed in that very hour.

anchor.BSB: Take courage, daughter, your faith has healed you.

MAT_9_24.speaker: Jesus
MAT_9_24.normalized: go away the girl is not dead but asleep

translations.NRSVUE: he said, “Go away, for the girl is not dead but sleeping.” And they laughed at him.

translations.LAMSA: And he said to them, That is enough; for the little girl is not dead, but she is asleep; and they laughed at him.

anchor.BSB: Go away. The girl is not dead, but asleep.

MAT_9_28.speaker: Jesus
MAT_9_28.normalized: do you believe that i am able to do this

translations.NRSVUE: When he entered the house, the blind men came to him, and Jesus said to them, “Do you have faith that I can do this?” They said to him, “Yes, Lord.”
translations.DBH: 28 And, when he came into the house, the blind men approached him, and Jesus says to them, “Do you have faith that I am able to do this?” They say to him, “Yes, Lord.”
translations.LAMSA: And when he came into the house, the same blind men came up to him. Jesus said to them, Do you believe that I can do this? They said to him, Yes, our Lord.

anchor.BSB: Do you believe that I am able to do this?

MAT_9_29.speaker: Jesus
MAT_9_29.normalized: according to your faith will it be done to you

translations.NRSVUE: Then he touched their eyes and said, “According to your faith, let it be done to you.”
translations.DBH: 29 Then he touched their eyes, saying, “According to your faith, let it happen to you.”
translations.LAMSA: Then he touched their eyes and said, Let it be to you according to your faith.

anchor.BSB: According to your faith will it be done to you.

MAT_9_30.speaker: Jesus
MAT_9_30.normalized: see that no one finds out about this

translations.NRSVUE: And their eyes were opened. Then Jesus sternly ordered them, “See that no one knows of this.”
translations.DBH: 30 And their eyes were opened. And Jesus sternly commanded them, “See you let no one know of this.”
translations.LAMSA: And immediately their eyes were opened; and Jesus charged them and said, See that no one knows it.

anchor.BSB: See that no one finds out about this!

MAT_9_37.speaker: Jesus
MAT_9_37.normalized: the harvest is plentiful but the workers are few

translations.NRSVUE: Then he said to his disciples, “The harvest is plentiful, but the laborers are few;
translations.DBH: 37 Then he says to his disciples, “Indeed the harvest is abundant, but the laborers few;
translations.LAMSA: So he said to his disciples, The harvest is great, and the laborers are few;

anchor.BSB: The harvest is plentiful, but the workers are few.

MAT_9_38.speaker: Jesus
MAT_9_38.normalized: ask the lord of the harvest therefore to send out workers into his harvest

translations.NRSVUE: therefore ask the Lord of the harvest to send out laborers into his harvest.”
translations.DBH: 38 pray to the Lord of the harvest, therefore, that he may send forth laborers to his harvest.”
translations.LAMSA: Therefore urge the owner of the harvest to bring more laborers to his harvest.

anchor.BSB: Ask the Lord of the harvest, therefore, to send out workers into His harvest.

MAT_10_5.speaker: Jesus
MAT_10_5.normalized: do not go onto the road of the gentiles or enter any town of the samaritans

translations.NRSVUE: These twelve Jesus sent out with the following instructions: “Do not take a road leading to gentiles, and do not enter a Samaritan town,
translations.DBH: 5 These twelve Jesus sent forth, giving them orders, saying: “Do not go forth on a road of the gentiles, and do not enter into a city of the Samaritans;
translations.LAMSA: These twelve Jesus sent out, and charged them and said, Keep away from pagan practices, and do not enter a Samaritan city;

anchor.BSB: Do not go onto the road of the Gentiles or enter any town of the Samaritans.

MAT_10_6.speaker: Jesus
MAT_10_6.normalized: go rather to the lost sheep of israel

translations.NRSVUE: but go rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.
translations.DBH: 6 go rather to the lost sheep of the household of Israel.
translations.LAMSA: But above all, go to the sheep which are lost from the house of Israel.

anchor.BSB: Go rather to the lost sheep of Israel.

MAT_10_7.speaker: Jesus
MAT_10_7.normalized: as you go preach this message the kingdom of heaven is near

translations.NRSVUE: As you go, proclaim the good news, ‘The kingdom of heaven has come near.’
translations.DBH: 7 And make a proclamation as you go along, saying that the Kingdom of the heavens has drawn near.
translations.LAMSA: And as you go, preach and say, that the kingdom of heaven is near.

anchor.BSB: As you go, preach this message: 'The kingdom of heaven is near.

MAT_10_8.speaker: Jesus
MAT_10_8.normalized: heal the sick raise the dead cleanse the lepers drive out demons freely you have received freely give

translations.NRSVUE: Cure the sick; raise the dead; cleanse those with a skin disease; cast out demons. You received without payment; give without payment.
translations.DBH: 8 Heal the ailing, raise the dead, cleanse lepers, exorcize demons. You have received a free gift; give a free gift.
translations.LAMSA: Heal the sick, cleanse the lepers, cast out demons; freely you have received, freely give.

anchor.BSB: Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse the lepers, drive out demons. Freely you have received; freely give.

MAT_10_9.speaker: Jesus
MAT_10_9.normalized: do not take along any gold or silver or copper in your belts

translations.NRSVUE: Take no gold, or silver, or copper in your belts,
translations.DBH: 9 Do not provide yourself with gold or silver or brass in your girdles,
translations.LAMSA: Do not accumulate gold, nor silver, nor brass in your purses;

anchor.BSB: Do not take along any gold or silver or copper in your belts.

MAT_10_10.speaker: Jesus
MAT_10_10.normalized: take no bag for the road or second tunic or sandals or staff for the worker is worthy of his provisions

translations.NRSVUE: no bag for your journey, or two tunics, or sandals, or a staff, for laborers deserve their food.
translations.DBH: 10 nor with a leather pouch for the road, or two tunics, or sandals, or a staff. For the laborer earns his provisions.
translations.LAMSA: Nor a bag for the journey, nor two shirts and shoes, nor a staff; for a laborer is at least worthy of his food.

anchor.BSB: Take no bag for the road, or second tunic, or sandals, or staff; for the worker is worthy of his provisions.

MAT_10_11.speaker: Jesus
MAT_10_11.normalized: whatever town or village you enter find out who is worthy there and stay at his house until you move on

translations.NRSVUE: Whatever town or village you enter, find out who in it is worthy, and stay there until you leave.
translations.DBH: 11 And, whatever city or village you enter into, carefully ascertain who within it is a worthy man, and stay with him until you should depart.
translations.LAMSA: Whatever city or town you enter, ask who is trustworthy in it, and remain there until you leave.

anchor.BSB: Whatever town or village you enter, find out who is worthy there and stay at his house until you move on.

MAT_10_12.speaker: Jesus
MAT_10_12.normalized: as you enter the home greet its occupants

translations.NRSVUE: As you enter the house, greet it.
translations.DBH: 12 On entering the household, moreover, give it your salutation;
translations.LAMSA: And when you enter into the house, salute the family.

anchor.BSB: As you enter the home, greet its occupants.

MAT_10_13.speaker: Jesus
MAT_10_13.normalized: if the home is worthy let your peace rest on it but if it is not let your peace return to you

translations.NRSVUE: If the house is worthy, let your peace come upon it, but if it is not worthy, let your peace return to you.
translations.DBH: 13 and if indeed the household should be worthy, may your ‘Peace’ come upon it; but if it should be unworthy, may your ‘Peace’ revert back to you.
translations.LAMSA: And if the family is trustworthy, your salutation of peace shall come upon it; but if it is not trustworthy, your salutation shall return to you.

anchor.BSB: If the home is worthy, let your peace rest on it, but if it is not, let your peace return to you.

translations.LAMSA: Whoever will not welcome you, and will not listen to your words, when you leave the house or the village, shake off the sand from your feet.

anchor.BSB: And if anyone will not welcome you or heed your words, shake the dust off your feet when you leave that home or town.

anchor.BSB: Behold, I am sending you out like sheep among wolves; therefore be as shrewd as snakes and as innocent as doves.

translations.LAMSA: But be careful of men; for they will deliver you up to the courts, and they will scourge you in their synagogues;

anchor.BSB: But beware of men, for they will hand you over to their councils and flog you in their synagogues.

translations.LAMSA: And they will bring you before the presence of governors and kings for my sake, as a testimony to them and to the Gentiles.

translations.LAMSA: But when they deliver you up, do not worry as to how or what you will speak; for it will be given to you in that very hour what you are to speak.

translations.LAMSA: Brother will deliver up his own brother to death, and father his son; and children will rise up against their parents and put them to death.

translations.LAMSA: No disciple is more important than his teacher, and no servant than his master.

anchor.BSB: A disciple is not above his teacher, nor a servant above his master.

translations.LAMSA: Therefore do not be afraid of them; for there is nothing covered that will not be uncovered, and hidden that will not be known.

translations.LAMSA: For I have come to set a man against his father, and a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.

translations.LAMSA: Whoever loves father or mother more than me is not worthy of me; and whoever loves son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me.

MAT_10_38.normalized: and anyone who does not take up his cross and follow me is not worthy of me

translations.DBH: 38 And whoever does not take up his cross and follow after me is not worthy of
translations.LAMSA: And whoever does not take up his cross and follow me is not worthy of me.

translations.NRSVUE: The good person brings good things out of a good treasure, and the evil person brings evil things out of an evil treasure.
translations.DBH: 35 The good man issues good things out of his good stores, and the wicked man issues wicked things out of his wicked stores.
translations.LAMSA: good man brings out good things from good treasures, and a bad man brings out bad things from bad treasures.

anchor.BSB: The good man brings good things out of his good store of treasure, and the evil man brings evil things out of his evil store of treasure.

MAT_12_36.speaker: Jesus
MAT_12_36.normalized: but i tell you that men will give an account on the day of judgment for every careless word they have spoken

translations.NRSVUE: I tell you, on the day of judgment you will have to give an account for every careless word you utter,
translations.DBH: 36 But I tell you that every idle word that men will speak, they will render an account of it on the day of judgment;
translations.LAMSA: For I say to you, that for every foolish word which men speak, they will have to answer for it on the day of judgment.

anchor.BSB: But I tell you that men will give an account on the day of judgment for every careless word they have spoken.

MAT_12_37.speaker: Jesus
MAT_12_37.normalized: for by your words you will be acquitted and by your words you will be condemned

translations.NRSVUE: for by your words you will be justified, and by your words you will be condemned.”
translations.DBH: 37 for by your words you shall be vindicated, and by your words you shall be condemned.”
translations.LAMSA: For by your words you shall be justified, and by your words you shall be found guilty.

anchor.BSB: For by your words you will be acquitted, and by your words you will be condemned.

MAT_12_39.speaker: Jesus
MAT_12_39.normalized: a wicked and adulterous generation demands a sign but none will be given it except the sign of the prophet jonah

translations.NRSVUE: But he answered them, “An evil and adulterous generation asks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except the sign of the prophet Jonah.
translations.DBH: 39 But in reply he said to them, “A wicked and adulterous generation seeks a sign and a sign shall not be given it, except the sign of the prophet Jonah.
translations.LAMSA: But he answered and said to them, An evil and adulterous generation wants a sign; and no sign will be given to it, except the sign of the prophet Jonah.

anchor.BSB: A wicked and adulterous generation demands a sign, but none will be given it except the sign of the prophet Jonah.

MAT_12_40.speaker: Jesus
MAT_12_40.normalized: for as jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the great fish so the son of man will be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth

translations.NRSVUE: For just as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the sea monster, so for three days and three nights the Son of Man will be in the heart of the earth.
translations.DBH: 40 For, just as Jonah was in the bowels of the sea-monster three days and three nights, so will the Son of Man be in the heart of the earth three days and three nights.
translations.LAMSA: For as Jonah was in the whale’s belly three days and three nights, so the Son of man will be in the heart of the earth, three days and three nights.

anchor.BSB: For as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the great fish, so the Son of Man will be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.

MAT_12_41.speaker: Jesus
MAT_12_41.normalized: the men of nineveh will stand at the judgment with this generation and condemn it for they repented at the preaching of jonah and now one greater than jonah is here

translations.NRSVUE: The people of Nineveh will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because they repented at the proclamation of Jonah, and indeed something greater than Jonah is here!
translations.DBH: 41 The men of Nineveh will stand up with this generation at the judgment and will condemn it; because at Jonah’s proclamation they changed their hearts, and look: Something more than Jonah is here.
translations.LAMSA: Even the men of Nineveh will rise up in judgment with this generation, and find it guilty; for they repented through the preaching of Jonah, and behold, a greater than Jonah is here.

anchor.BSB: The men of Nineveh will stand at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for they repented at the preaching of Jonah, and now One greater than Jonah is here.

MAT_12_42.speaker: Jesus
MAT_12_42.normalized: the queen of the south will rise at the judgment with this generation and condemn it for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of solomon and now one greater than solomon is here

translations.NRSVUE: The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because she came from the ends of the earth to listen to the wisdom of Solomon, and indeed something greater than Solomon is here!
translations.DBH: 42 The Queen of the South will be raised with this generation at the judgment and will condemn it; because she came from the ends of the earth to listen to Solomon’s wisdom, and look: Something more than Solomon is here.
translations.LAMSA: The queen of the south will rise up in judgment with this generation, and find it guilty; for she came from the far ends of the earth that she might hear Solomon’s wisdom, and behold a greater than Solomon is here.

anchor.BSB: The Queen of the South will rise at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and now One greater than Solomon is here.

MAT_12_43.speaker: Jesus
MAT_12_43.normalized: when an unclean spirit comes out of a man it passes through arid places seeking rest and does not find it

translations.NRSVUE: “When the unclean spirit has gone out of a person, it wanders through waterless regions looking for a resting place, but it finds none.
translations.DBH: 43 And when the unclean spirit comes out of the man it wanders through dry places seeking rest, and does not find it.
translations.LAMSA: When an unclean spirit goes out of a man, it travels in places where there is no water, and seeks rest, and does not find it.

anchor.BSB: When an unclean spirit comes out of a man, it passes through arid places seeking rest and does not find it.

MAT_12_44.speaker: Jesus
MAT_12_44.normalized: then it says i will return to the house i left on its return it finds the house vacant swept clean and put in order

translations.NRSVUE: Then it says, ‘I will return to my house from which I came.’ When it returns, it finds it empty, swept, and put in order.
translations.DBH: 44 Then it says, ‘I shall go back into my house, from which I came out’; and coming it finds it standing idle, swept clean and furnished in good order.
translations.LAMSA: Then it says, I will return to my own house from whence I came out; so it comes back and finds it empty, warm, and well furnished.

anchor.BSB: Then it says, 'I will return to the house I left.' On its return, it finds the house vacant, swept clean, and put in order.

MAT_12_45.speaker: Jesus
MAT_12_45.normalized: then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more wicked than itself and they go in and dwell there and the final plight of that man is worse than the first so will it be with this wicked generation

translations.NRSVUE: Then it goes and brings along seven other spirits more evil than itself, and they enter and live there, and the last state of that person is worse than the first. So will it be also with this evil generation.”
translations.DBH: 45 Then it goes off and brings along with itself seven other spirits, more evil than itself, and enters in and takes up residence there; and the man’s last circumstances turn out worse than the first. So it shall also be for this wicked generation.”
translations.LAMSA: Then it goes away and brings with it seven other spirits worse than itself, and they enter and live in it; and the end of that man becomes worse than at first. Such will happen to this evil generation.

anchor.BSB: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more wicked than itself, and they go in and dwell there. And the final plight of that man is worse than the first. So will it be with this wicked generation.

MAT_12_48.speaker: Jesus
MAT_12_48.normalized: who is my mother and who are my brothers

translations.NRSVUE: But to the one who had told him this, Jesus replied, “Who is my mother, and who are my brothers?”
translations.DBH: 48 But in reply he said to the one who had spoken to him, “Who is my mother, and who are my brothers?”
translations.LAMSA: But he answered and said to him who told him, Who is my mother and who are my brothers?

anchor.BSB: Who is My mother, and who are My brothers?

MAT_12_49.speaker: Jesus
MAT_12_49.normalized: here are my mother and my brothers

translations.NRSVUE: And pointing to his disciples, he said, “Here are my mother and my brothers!
translations.DBH: 49 And stretching out a hand toward his disciples he said, “Look: my mother and my brothers.
translations.LAMSA: And he pointed his hand to his disciples and said, Behold my mother, and behold my brothers.

anchor.BSB: Here are My mother and My brothers.

MAT_12_50.speaker: Jesus
MAT_12_50.normalized: for whoever does the will of my father in heaven is my brother and sister and mother

translations.NRSVUE: For whoever does the will of my Father in heaven is my brother and sister and mother.”
translations.DBH: 50 For whoever does the will of my Father in the heavens, that one is my brother and sister and mother.”
translations.LAMSA: For whoever does the will of my Father in heaven, he is my brother and my sister and my mother.

anchor.BSB: For whoever does the will of My Father in heaven is My brother and sister and mother.

MAT_13_3.speaker: Jesus
MAT_13_3.normalized: a farmer went out to sow his seed

translations.NRSVUE: And he told them many things in parables, saying: “Listen! A sower went out to sow.
translations.DBH: 3 And he told them many things in parables, saying, “Look: A sower went out to sow.
translations.LAMSA: And he spoke many things to them in parables, and said, Behold the sower went out to sow;

anchor.BSB: A farmer went out to sow his seed.

MAT_13_4.speaker: Jesus
MAT_13_4.normalized: and as he was sowing some seed fell along the path and the birds came and devoured it

translations.NRSVUE: And as he sowed, some seeds fell on a path, and the birds came and ate them up.
translations.DBH: 4 And, as he was sowing seeds, some of course fell beside the path and birds came and devoured them.
translations.LAMSA: And when he had sown, some seed fell on the roadside, and the fowls came and ate it.

anchor.BSB: And as he was sowing, some seed fell along the path, and the birds came and devoured it.

MAT_13_5.speaker: Jesus
MAT_13_5.normalized: some fell on rocky ground where it did not have much soil it sprang up quickly because the soil was shallow

translations.NRSVUE: Other seeds fell on rocky ground, where they did not have much soil, and they sprang up quickly, since they had no depth of soil.
translations.DBH: 5 Others, however, fell upon stony places where there was not much soil, and it sprang up instantly because there was no depth to the soil;
translations.LAMSA: Other fell upon the rock, where there was not sufficient soil; and it sprang up earlier because the ground was not deep enough;

anchor.BSB: Some fell on rocky ground, where it did not have much soil. It sprang up quickly because the soil was shallow.

MAT_13_6.normalized: but when the sun rose the seedlings were scorched and they withered because they had no root

translations.DBH: 6 but when the sun had risen it was parched, and because it had no root it withered away.
translations.LAMSA: But when the sun shone, it was scorched, and because it had no root, it dried up;

anchor.BSB: But when the sun rose, the seedlings were scorched, and they withered because they had no root.

MAT_13_7.normalized: other seed fell among thorns which grew up and choked the seedlings

translations.NRSVUE: Other seeds fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up and choked them.
translations.DBH: 7 But still others fell upon thorns, and the thorns grew up and throttled them.
translations.LAMSA: And other fell among thistles, and the thistles sprung up and choked it.

anchor.BSB: Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up and choked the seedlings.

MAT_13_8.speaker: Jesus
MAT_13_8.normalized: still other seed fell on good soil and produced a crop a hundredfold sixtyfold or thirtyfold

translations.NRSVUE: Other seeds fell on good soil and brought forth grain, some a hundredfold, some sixty, some thirty.

translations.LAMSA: And other fell in good soil, and bore fruit, some one hundred, and some sixty, and some thirty.

anchor.BSB: Still other seed fell on good soil and produced a crop-a hundredfold, sixtyfold, or thirtyfold.

MAT_13_9.speaker: Jesus
MAT_13_9.normalized: he who has ears let him hear

translations.NRSVUE: If you have ears, hear!”
translations.DBH: 9 Let him who has ears listen.”
translations.LAMSA: He who has ears to hear, let him hear.

anchor.BSB: He who has ears, let him hear.

MAT_13_11.speaker: Jesus

translations.DBH: 11 And in reply he said, “Because it has been granted to you to know the mysteries of the Kingdom of the heavens, but it has not been granted to them.
translations.LAMSA: He answered and said to them, Because to you it is granted to know the mystery of the kingdom of heaven, but it is not granted to them.

anchor.BSB: The knowledge of the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven has been given to you, but not to them.

MAT_13_12.speaker: Jesus
MAT_13_12.normalized: whoever has will be given more and he will have an abundance whoever does not have even what he has will be taken away from him

anchor.BSB: Whoever has will be given more, and he will have an abundance. Whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.

MAT_13_13.speaker: Jesus
MAT_13_13.normalized: this is why i speak to them in parables though seeing they do not see though hearing they do not hear or understand

translations.DBH: 13 Hence I speak to them in parables, because seeing they do not see, and hearing they neither hear nor understand.
translations.LAMSA: This is the reason I speak to them in figures, because they see and yet cannot perceive; and they hear and yet do not listen, nor do they understand.

anchor.BSB: This is why I speak to them in parables: 'Though seeing, they do not see; though hearing, they do not hear or understand.

MAT_13_14.speaker: Jesus
MAT_13_14.normalized: in them the prophecy of isaiah is fulfilled you will be ever hearing but never understanding you will be ever seeing but never perceiving

translations.LAMSA: And in them is fulfilled the prophecy of Isaiah who said, Hearing you will hear, but you will not understand; and seeing you will see, but you will not know.

anchor.BSB: In them the prophecy of Isaiah is fulfilled: 'You will be ever hearing but never understanding; you will be ever seeing but never perceiving.

MAT_13_15.speaker: Jesus
MAT_13_15.normalized: for this people s heart has grown callous they hardly hear with their ears and they have closed their eyes otherwise they might see with their eyes hear with their ears understand with their hearts and turn and i would heal them

translations.NRSVUE: For this people’s heart has grown dull, and their ears are hard of hearing, and they have shut their eyes, so that they might not look with their eyes, and hear with their ears and understand with their heart and turn— and I would heal them.’
translations.DBH: 15 For this people’s heart has grown crass, and they have listened with their ears grudgingly, and they have closed their eyes, so that it may never happen that they see with their eyes and hear with their ears and understand with the heart and turn back, and I shall heal them.’
translations.LAMSA: For the heart of this people has become hardened, and their ears hear heavily, and their eyes are dull; so that they cannot see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and understand with their hearts; let them return, and I will heal them.

anchor.BSB: For this people's heart has grown callous; they hardly hear with their ears, and they have closed their eyes. Otherwise they might see with their eyes, hear with their ears, understand with their hearts, and turn, and I would heal them.

MAT_13_16.normalized: but blessed are your eyes because they see and your ears because they hear

translations.NRSVUE: “But blessed are your eyes, for they see, and your ears, for they hear.
translations.DBH: 16 But blissful are your eyes because they see, and your ears because they hear.
translations.LAMSA: But as for you, blessed are your eyes for they see; and your ears for they hear.

anchor.BSB: But blessed are your eyes because they see, and your ears because they hear.

MAT_13_17.speaker: Jesus
MAT_13_17.normalized: for truly i tell you many prophets and righteous men longed to see what you see but did not see it and to hear what you hear but did not hear it

translations.NRSVUE: Truly I tell you, many prophets and righteous people longed to see what you see but did not see it and to hear what you hear but did not hear it.
translations.DBH: 17 For, amen, I tell you that many prophets and upright men yearned to see the things you see, and did not see, and to hear the things you hear, and did not hear.
translations.LAMSA: For truly I say to you, a great many prophets and righteous men have longed to see what you see, and did not see it; and to hear what you hear, and did not hear it.

anchor.BSB: For truly I tell you, many prophets and righteous men longed to see what you see but did not see it, and to hear what you hear but did not hear it.

MAT_13_18.speaker: Jesus
MAT_13_18.normalized: consider then the parable of the sower

translations.NRSVUE: “Hear, then, the parable of the sower.
translations.DBH: 18 Listen, therefore, to the parable of the sower.
translations.LAMSA: Now you listen to the parable of the seed.

anchor.BSB: Consider, then, the parable of the sower:

MAT_13_19.speaker: Jesus
MAT_13_19.normalized: when anyone hears the message of the kingdom but does not understand it the evil one comes and snatches away what was sown in his heart this is the seed sown along the path

translations.NRSVUE: When anyone hears the word of the kingdom and does not understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away what is sown in the heart; this is what was sown on the path.

translations.LAMSA: Whoever hears the word of the kingdom and does not understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away the word which has been sown in his heart. This is that which was sown on the roadside.

anchor.BSB: When anyone hears the message of the kingdom but does not understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away what was sown in his heart. This is the seed sown along the path.

MAT_13_20.speaker: Jesus
MAT_13_20.normalized: the seed sown on rocky ground is the one who hears the word and at once receives it with joy

translations.LAMSA: That which was sown upon the rock, this is he who hears the word, and immediately accepts it with joy;

anchor.BSB: The seed sown on rocky ground is the one who hears the word and at once receives it with joy.

MAT_13_21.speaker: Jesus
MAT_13_21.normalized: but since he has no root he remains for only a season when trouble or persecution comes because of the word he quickly falls away

translations.LAMSA: But it has no root in him, except for a while; and when trouble or persecution comes because of the word, he immediately stumbles.

anchor.BSB: But since he has no root, he remains for only a season. When trouble or persecution comes because of the word, he quickly falls away.

MAT_13_22.speaker: Jesus

translations.NRSVUE: As for what was sown among thorns, this is the one who hears the word, but the cares of this age and the lure of wealth choke the word, and it yields nothing.
translations.DBH: 22 And what is sown among the thorns: this is the one who hears the word, and the anxiety of the age and the beguilement of riches throttle the word, and it becomes fruitless.
translations.LAMSA: That which was sown among thistles, this is he who hears the word, but worldly thoughts and the deception caused by riches choke the word, and it becomes fruitless.

anchor.BSB: The seed sown among the thorns is the one who hears the word, but the worries of this life and the deceitfulness of wealth choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.

MAT_13_23.speaker: Jesus

translations.LAMSA: That which was sown upon good soil, this is he who hears my word, and understands it, so he bears fruit and produces some one hundred, and some sixty, and some thirty.

anchor.BSB: But the seed sown on good soil is the one who hears the word and understands it. He indeed bears fruit and produces a crop-a hundredfold, sixtyfold, or thirtyfold.

anchor.BSB: The kingdom of heaven is like a man who sowed good seed in his field.

translations.LAMSA: And when the men slept, his enemy came and sowed tares among the wheat, and went away.

anchor.BSB: But while everyone was asleep, his enemy came and sowed weeds among the wheat, and slipped away.

MAT_13_26.normalized: when the wheat sprouted and bore grain then the weeds also appeared

translations.LAMSA: But when the blade sprang up and bore fruit, then the tares also appeared.

anchor.BSB: When the wheat sprouted and bore grain, then the weeds also appeared.

translations.DBH: 27 And the householder’s slaves, approaching, said to him, ‘Lord, did you not sow good seed in your field? Where have the darnel-weeds it contains come from?’
translations.LAMSA: So the servants of the landowner came and said to him, Our lord, behold, did you not sow good seed in your field; whence did the tares come into it?

anchor.BSB: The owner's servants came to him and said, 'Sir, didn't you sow good seed in your field? Where then did the weeds come from?

MAT_13_28.speaker: Jesus
MAT_13_28.normalized: an enemy did this he replied so the servants asked him do you want us to go and pull them up

translations.NRSVUE: He answered, ‘An enemy has done this.’ The slaves said to him, ‘Then do you want us to go and gather them?’
translations.DBH: 28 And he said to them, ‘Someone who is an enemy did this.’ So the slaves say to him, ‘Do you wish then that we should go out and gather them?’
translations.LAMSA: He said to them, An enemy did this; his servants then said to him, Do you want us to go and pick them out?

anchor.BSB: An enemy did this,' he replied. So the servants asked him, 'Do you want us to go and pull them up?

MAT_13_29.speaker: Jesus
MAT_13_29.normalized: no he said if you pull the weeds now you might uproot the wheat with them

translations.NRSVUE: But he replied, ‘No, for in gathering the weeds you would uproot the wheat along with them.
translations.DBH: 29 But he says, ‘No, lest in gathering the darnel-weeds you should uproot the grain along with them.
translations.LAMSA: But he said to them, It might happen that while you were picking out the tares, you might uproot with them also the wheat.

anchor.BSB: No,' he said, 'if you pull the weeds now, you might uproot the wheat with them.

MAT_13_30.speaker: Jesus
MAT_13_30.normalized: let both grow together until the harvest at that time i will tell the harvesters first collect the weeds and tie them in bundles to be burned then gather the wheat into my barn

translations.NRSVUE: Let both of them grow together until the harvest, and at harvest time I will tell the reapers, Collect the weeds first and bind them in bundles to be burned, but gather the wheat into my barn.’ ”
translations.DBH: 30 Let them both grow up together until the harvest; and at the time of the harvest I shall tell the reapers, “First gather the darnel-weeds and tie them in sheaves in order to burn them; but gather the grain into my granary.”’”
translations.LAMSA: Let them both grow together until the harvest; and at the harvest season, I will say to the reapers, Pick out first the tares, and bind them into bundles to be burned; but gather the wheat into my barns.

anchor.BSB: Let both grow together until the harvest. At that time I will tell the harvesters: First collect the weeds and tie them in bundles to be burned; then gather the wheat into my barn.

MAT_13_31.speaker: Jesus
MAT_13_31.normalized: the kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that a man took and planted in his field

translations.NRSVUE: He put before them another parable: “The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that someone took and sowed in his field;
translations.DBH: 31 He set another parable before them, saying, “The Kingdom of the heavens is like mustard seed that a man took and sowed in his field,
translations.LAMSA: He related another parable to them, and said, The kingdom of heaven is like a grain of mustard seed, which a man took and sowed in his field.

anchor.BSB: The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that a man took and planted in his field.

translations.DBH: 23 Alas for you, scribes and Pharisees, charlatans, because you tithe a tenth of the mint and the dill and the cumin, and have neglected the weightier things of the law, the judgment and the mercy and the faith; yet these things you ought to have done, while also not neglecting those others.
translations.LAMSA: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for you take tithes on mint, dill, and cummin, and you have overlooked the more important matters of the law, such as justice, mercy, and trustworthiness. MAT_25_14.normalized: for it is just like a man going on a journey who called his servants and entrusted them with his possessions

translations.NRSVUE: “For it is as if a man, going on a journey, summoned his slaves and entrusted his property to them;
translations.DBH: 14 For just as a man leaving home on a journey summoned his own slaves and handed his possessions over to them, 14 Then one of the twelve, the one named Judas Iscariot, going to the chief priests, 14 And he did not answer him, not a single word, so that the governor was greatly astonished. 14 And if this is heard of by the governor we shall prevail upon him and see to it that you are free from trouble.”
translations.LAMSA: It is just like a man who went on a journey, who called his servants and put his wealth in their charge.

anchor.BSB: For it is just like a man going on a journey, who called his servants and entrusted them with his possessions.

MAT_25_15.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_15.normalized: to one he gave five talents to another two talents and to another one talent each according to his own ability and he went on his journey

translations.NRSVUE: to one he gave five talents, to another two, to another one, to each according to his ability. Then he went away. At once
translations.DBH: 15 and gave five talents to one, and two to another, and one to another, to each according to his peculiar ability, and left home on his journey— 15 said, “What are you willing to give me, and I shall hand him over to you?” And they paid him thirty silver pieces. 15 Now, for the festival it was the governor’s custom to release to the crowd one prisoner, whomever they wished. 15 And taking the silver they did as they were instructed. And this is the tale that has been spread about by the Judaeans to this day.
translations.LAMSA: To one he gave five talents, to one two, to another one; to each one according to his ability; and immediately he went on a journey.

anchor.BSB: To one he gave five talents, to another two talents, and to another one talent-each according to his own ability. And he went on his journey.

MAT_25_16.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_16.normalized: the servant who had received the five talents went at once and put them to work and gained five more

translations.NRSVUE: the one who had received the five talents went off and traded with them and made five more talents.
translations.DBH: 16 Immediately, the one who received five talents employed them in trade and gained another five; 16 And from then on he sought a good opportunity, so that he might hand him over. 16 And they had at that time a notable prisoner named bar-Abbas. 16 But the eleven disciples went into Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus appointed them,
translations.LAMSA: The one who received five talents then went and traded with them, and he earned five others.

anchor.BSB: The servant who had received the five talents went at once and put them to work and gained five more.

MAT_25_17.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_17.normalized: likewise the one with the two talents gained two more

translations.NRSVUE: In the same way, the one who had the two talents made two more talents.
translations.DBH: 17 similarly, the one who had two gained another two; 17 When therefore they were assembled Pilate said to them, “Whom do you wish I should release to you, bar-Abbas or Jesus, who is called the Anointed?” 17 and seeing him they prostrated themselves; but some doubted.
translations.LAMSA: Likewise the second one, he gained by trading gained two others.

anchor.BSB: Likewise, the one with the two talents gained two more.

MAT_25_18.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_18.normalized: but the servant who had received the one talent went off dug a hole in the ground and hid his master s money

translations.DBH: 18 but the one who received one went away, dug into the ground, and hid his lord’s silver. 18 And he said, “Go into the city to a certain man and say to him, ‘The teacher says, “My appointed time is near; I am observing the Passover at your place, along with my disciples.”’” 18 For he knew that they had handed him over through malice. 18 And, approaching, Jesus spoke to them, saying, “All power in heaven and on earth has been given to me.
translations.LAMSA: But he who received one, went and dug in the ground, and hid his lord’s money.

anchor.BSB: But the servant who had received the one talent went off, dug a hole in the ground, and hid his master's money.

MAT_25_19.normalized: after a long time the master of those servants returned and settled accounts with them

anchor.BSB: After a long time the master of those servants returned and settled accounts with them.

MAT_25_20.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_20.normalized: the servant who had received the five talents came and presented five more master he said you entrusted me with five talents see i have gained five more

translations.DBH: 20 And, approaching, the one receiving the five talents brought the other five talents forward, saying, ‘Lord, you handed over five talents to me; look, I gained another five talents.’ 20 And when evening came he reclined at table with the twelve. 20 But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowds that they should ask for bar-Abbas and should destroy Jesus. 20 teaching them to observe everything that I have commanded you; and see: I am with you every day until the consummation of the age.” The Gospel

anchor.BSB: His master replied, 'Well done, good and faithful servant! You have been faithful with a few things; I will put you in charge of many things. Enter into the joy of your master!

translations.LAMSA: His lord said to him, Well done, good and reliable servant, you have been faithful over a little, I will appoint you over much; enter into your master’s joy.

MAT_25_24.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_24.normalized: finally the servant who had received the one talent came and said master i knew that you are a hard man reaping where you have not sown and gathering where you have not scattered seed

translations.LAMSA: Then the one who received one talent also came up, and he said, My lord, I knew that you are a hard man, and you reap where you did not sow, and gather where you did not scatter.

LUK_10_11.normalized: even the dust of your town that clings to our feet we wipe off as a testimony against you yet be sure of this the kingdom of god is near

anchor.BSB: I praise You, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because You have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, for this was well-pleasing in Your sight.
