LUK_11_21.normalized: when a strong man fully armed guards his house his possessions are secure

translations.NRSVUE: When a strong man, fully armed, guards his castle, his property is safe.
translations.DBH: 21 When a strong man, well-armed, keeps guard over his own courtyard, his possessions rest peacefully;
translations.LAMSA: When a strong man is armed and keeps watch over his courtyard, his property is safe;

anchor.BSB: When a strong man, fully armed, guards his house, his possessions are secure.

LUK_11_22.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_22.normalized: but when someone stronger attacks and overpowers him he takes away the armor in which the man trusted and then he divides up his plunder

translations.NRSVUE: But when one stronger than he attacks him and overpowers him, he takes away his armor in which he trusted and divides his plunder.
translations.DBH: 22 but, when someone stronger than he attacks and vanquishes him, he strips away his panoply, on which he had relied, and divides up the weapons despoiled from him.
translations.LAMSA: But if there should come one who is stronger than he, he will conquer him and take away his armor in which he trusted and divide his spoil.

anchor.BSB: But when someone stronger attacks and overpowers him, he takes away the armor in which the man trusted, and then he divides up his plunder.

LUK_11_23.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_23.normalized: he who is not with me is against me and he who does not gather with me scatters

translations.NRSVUE: Whoever is not with me is against me, and whoever does not gather with me scatters.
translations.DBH: 23 Whoever is not with me is against me, and whoever does not gather together with me scatters.
translations.LAMSA: He who is not with me is against me; and he who does not gather with me will scatter.

anchor.BSB: He who is not with Me is against Me, and he who does not gather with Me scatters.

LUK_11_24.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_24.normalized: when an unclean spirit comes out of a man it passes through arid places seeking rest and does not find it then it says i will return to the house i left

translations.NRSVUE: “When the unclean spirit has gone out of a person, it wanders through waterless regions looking for a resting place, but not finding any it says, ‘I will return to my house from which I came.’
translations.DBH: 24 When the impure spirit departs from the man, it passes through all the dry places seeking rest, and finds none. It [then] says, ‘I shall return to my house, from which I departed.’
translations.LAMSA: When an unclean spirit is gone out of a man, it goes away and travels in places where there is no water, to seek rest; and when it finds it not, it says, I will return to my own house from whence I came.

anchor.BSB: When an unclean spirit comes out of a man, it passes through arid places seeking rest and does not find it. Then it says, 'I will return to the house I left.

LUK_11_25.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_25.normalized: on its return it finds the house swept clean and put in order

translations.NRSVUE: When it returns, it finds it swept and put in order.
translations.DBH: 25 And coming it finds it swept clean and furnished in good order.
translations.LAMSA: And if it should come and find it warm and well furnished,

anchor.BSB: On its return, it finds the house swept clean and put in order.

LUK_11_26.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_26.normalized: then it goes and brings seven other spirits more wicked than itself and they go in and dwell there and the final plight of that man is worse than the first

translations.NRSVUE: Then it goes and brings seven other spirits more evil than itself, and they enter and live there, and the last state of that person is worse than the first.”
translations.DBH: 26 Then it goes and brings along seven other spirits more wicked than itself, and entering in it dwells there, and the man’s last circumstances become worse than the first.”
translations.LAMSA: Then it goes away and brings seven other spirits worse than itself; and they enter and dwell there; and the end of that man will become worse than the beginning.

anchor.BSB: Then it goes and brings seven other spirits more wicked than itself, and they go in and dwell there. And the final plight of that man is worse than the first.

LUK_11_28.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_28.normalized: blessed rather are those who hear the word of god and obey it

translations.NRSVUE: But he said, “Blessed rather are those who hear the word of God and obey it!”
translations.DBH: 28 But he said, “Blissful, rather, those hearing God’s word and keeping it.”
translations.LAMSA: He said to her, Blessed are they who hear the word of God and keep it.

anchor.BSB: Blessed rather are those who hear the word of God and obey it.

LUK_11_29.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_29.normalized: this is a wicked generation it demands a sign but none will be given it except the sign of jonah

translations.NRSVUE: When the crowds were increasing, he began to say, “This generation is an evil generation; it asks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except the sign of Jonah.
translations.DBH: 29 And as the crowds pressed in upon him he began to say, “This generation is a wicked generation; it seeks a sign, and a sign shall not be given to it, except the sign of Jonah [the prophet].
translations.LAMSA: And when the people were gathering, he began to say, This evil generation wants a sign; and no sign will be given to it except the sign of the prophet Jonah.

anchor.BSB: This is a wicked generation. It demands a sign, but none will be given it except the sign of Jonah.

LUK_11_30.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_30.normalized: for as jonah was a sign to the ninevites so the son of man will be a sign to this generation

translations.NRSVUE: For just as Jonah became a sign to the people of Nineveh, so the Son of Man will be to this generation.
translations.DBH: 30 For just as Jonah became a sign to the Ninevites, so also will the Son of Man to this generation.
translations.LAMSA: For as Jonah was a sign to the Ninevites, so also will the Son of man be to this generation.

anchor.BSB: For as Jonah was a sign to the Ninevites, so the Son of Man will be a sign to this generation.

LUK_11_31.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_31.normalized: the queen of the south will rise at the judgment with the men of this generation and condemn them for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of solomon and now one greater than solomon is here

translations.NRSVUE: The queen of the South will rise at the judgment with the people of this generation and condemn them, because she came from the ends of the earth to listen to the wisdom of Solomon, and indeed, something greater than Solomon is here!
translations.DBH: 31 The Queen of the South will be raised at the judgment along with this generation and will condemn them; because she came from the farthest reaches of this earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and see: Something greater than Solomon is here.
translations.LAMSA: The queen of the south will rise up in judgment with the men of this generation and condemn them; for she came from the far ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and behold, a greater than Solomon is here.

anchor.BSB: The Queen of the South will rise at the judgment with the men of this generation and condemn them; for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and now One greater than Solomon is here.

LUK_11_32.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_32.normalized: the men of nineveh will stand at the judgment with this generation and condemn it for they repented at the preaching of jonah and now one greater than jonah is here

translations.NRSVUE: The people of Nineveh will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because they repented at the proclamation of Jonah, and indeed, something greater than Jonah is here!
translations.DBH: 32 Ninevite men will rise at the judgment along with this generation and will condemn it; because at the proclamation of Jonah they changed their hearts, and see: Something greater than Jonah is here.
translations.LAMSA: The men of Nineveh will rise up in judgment against this generation and condemn it; for they repented at the preaching of Jonah; and behold, a greater than Jonah is here.

anchor.BSB: The men of Nineveh will stand at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for they repented at the preaching of Jonah, and now One greater than Jonah is here.

LUK_11_33.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_33.normalized: no one lights a lamp and puts it in a cellar or under a basket instead he sets it on a stand so those who enter can see the light

translations.NRSVUE: “No one after lighting a lamp puts it in a cellar or under a bushel basket; rather, one puts it on the lampstand so that those who enter may see the light.
translations.DBH: 33 No one having lit a lamp sets it in a hidden place or under the dry-goods basket, but rather on the lampstand, so that those coming in may see the radiance.
translations.LAMSA: No man lights a lamp and puts it in a hidden place or under a basket, but on a lamp holder, so that those who enter may see its light.

anchor.BSB: No one lights a lamp and puts it in a cellar or under a basket. Instead, he sets it on a stand, so those who enter can see the light.

LUK_11_34.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_34.normalized: your eye is the lamp of your body when your eyes are good your whole body also is full of light but when they are bad your body is full of darkness

translations.NRSVUE: Your eye is the lamp of your body. If your eye is healthy, your whole body is full of light, but if it is unhealthy, your body is full of darkness.
translations.DBH: 34 The body’s lamp is your eye. When your eye is pure, your whole body is luminous; but when it is baleful, your body is dark also.
translations.LAMSA: The lamp of your body is your eye; when therefore your eye is bright, your whole body will also be lighted; but if it is diseased, your whole body will also be dark.

anchor.BSB: Your eye is the lamp of your body. When your eyes are good, your whole body also is full of light. But when they are bad, your body is full of darkness.

LUK_11_35.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_35.normalized: be careful then that the light within you is not darkness

translations.NRSVUE: Therefore consider whether the light in you is not darkness.
translations.DBH: 35 Keep watch, therefore, that the light in you is not darkness.
translations.LAMSA: Take heed, therefore, test the light which is in you be darkness.

anchor.BSB: Be careful, then, that the light within you is not darkness.

LUK_11_36.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_36.normalized: so if your whole body is full of light with no part of it in darkness you will be radiant as though a lamp were shining on you

translations.NRSVUE: But if your whole body is full of light, with no part of it in darkness, it will be as full of light as when a lamp gives you light with its rays.”
translations.DBH: 36 If, therefore, your whole body is luminous, having no part dark at all, it will be wholly luminous, as when the lamp illuminates you with its shining.”
translations.LAMSA: If your whole body is lighted and there is no part in it dark, the whole of it will give light, just as a lamp gives you light with its shining.

anchor.BSB: So if your whole body is full of light, with no part of it in darkness, you will be radiant, as though a lamp were shining on you.

LUK_11_39.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_39.normalized: now you pharisees clean the outside of the cup and dish but inside you are full of greed and wickedness

translations.NRSVUE: Then the Lord said to him, “Now you Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and of the dish, but inside you are full of greed and wickedness.
translations.DBH: 39 But the Lord said to him, “But you, the Pharisees, cleanse the outside of the cup and the dish, but your inside is full of spoliation and wickedness.
translations.LAMSA: And Jesus said to him, Now you Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and the dish; but within you are full of extortion and iniquity.

anchor.BSB: Now you Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and dish, but inside you are full of greed and wickedness.

LUK_11_40.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_40.normalized: you fools did not the one who made the outside make the inside as well

translations.NRSVUE: You fools! Did not the one who made the outside make the inside also?
translations.DBH: 40 Fools, did not the one who made the outside make the inside also?
translations.LAMSA: O you shortsighted, did not he who made the outside also make the inside?

anchor.BSB: You fools! Did not the One who made the outside make the inside as well?

LUK_11_41.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_41.normalized: but give as alms the things that are within you and behold everything will be clean for you

translations.NRSVUE: So give as alms those things that are within and then everything will be clean for you.
translations.DBH: 41 Instead, give away the things that are within as alms, and look: All things are pure to you.
translations.LAMSA: But give alms of what you have; and, behold, everything will be clean to you.

anchor.BSB: But give as alms the things that are within you, and behold, everything will be clean for you.

LUK_11_42.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_42.normalized: woe to you pharisees for you pay tithes of mint rue and every herb but you disregard justice and the love of god you should have practiced the latter without neglecting the former

translations.NRSVUE: “But woe to you Pharisees! For you tithe mint and rue and herbs of all kinds and neglect justice and the love of God; it is these you ought to have practiced, without neglecting the others.
translations.DBH: 42 But alas for you, the Pharisees, because you make the tithe of the mint and the rue and every herb, and neglect God’s judgment and love; but these it was necessary to practice, without neglecting those things also.
translations.LAMSA: But woe to you Pharisees! who take tithes of mint and dill and every kind of vegetable, but overlook justice and the love of God. These were necessary for you to have done, the very ones by no means to have been left undone.

anchor.BSB: Woe to you Pharisees! For you pay tithes of mint, rue, and every herb, but you disregard justice and the love of God. You should have practiced the latter without neglecting the former.

LUK_11_43.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_43.normalized: woe to you pharisees for you love the chief seats in the synagogues and the greetings in the marketplaces

translations.NRSVUE: Woe to you Pharisees! For you love to have the seat of honor in the synagogues and to be greeted with respect in the marketplaces.
translations.DBH: 43 Alas for you, the Pharisees, because you love the chief seat in the synagogues and the salutations in the marketplaces.
translations.LAMSA: Woe to you Pharisees! for you love chief seats in the synagogues and salutations in the streets.

anchor.BSB: Woe to you Pharisees! For you love the chief seats in the synagogues and the greetings in the marketplaces.

LUK_11_44.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_44.normalized: woe to you for you are like unmarked graves which men walk over without even noticing

translations.NRSVUE: Woe to you! For you are like unmarked graves on which people unknowingly walk.”
translations.DBH: 44 Alas for you, [scribes and Pharisees, you charlatans,] for you are like unseen graves, and the people walking over them do not know it.”
translations.LAMSA: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for you are like graves that cannot be recognized, and men walk over them and know it not.

anchor.BSB: Woe to you! For you are like unmarked graves, which men walk over without even noticing.

LUK_11_46.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_46.normalized: woe to you as well experts in the law for you weigh men down with heavy burdens but you yourselves will not lift a finger to lighten their load

translations.NRSVUE: And he said, “Woe also to you experts in the law! For you load people with burdens hard to bear, and you yourselves do not lift a finger to ease them.
translations.DBH: 46 And he said, “Alas also for you, the lawyers, because you burden men with unbearable burdens, while you yourselves do not apply a single one of your fingers to those burdens.
translations.LAMSA: But he said, Woe also to you, scribes! for you lay heavy burdens on men, and you yourselves do not touch these burdens even with one of your fingers.

anchor.BSB: Woe to you as well, experts in the law! For you weigh men down with heavy burdens, but you yourselves will not lift a finger to lighten their load.

LUK_11_47.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_47.normalized: woe to you for you build tombs for the prophets but it was your fathers who killed them

translations.NRSVUE: Woe to you! For you build the tombs of the prophets whom your ancestors killed.
translations.DBH: 47 Alas for you, because you build the tombs of the prophets, and your fathers killed them.
translations.LAMSA: Woe to you! for you build the tombs of prophets whom your fathers killed.

anchor.BSB: Woe to you! For you build tombs for the prophets, but it was your fathers who killed them.

LUK_11_48.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_48.normalized: so you are witnesses consenting to the deeds of your fathers they killed the prophets and you build their tombs

translations.NRSVUE: So you are witnesses and approve of the deeds of your ancestors, for they killed them, and you build their tombs.
translations.DBH: 48 You, therefore, are witnesses that you consent to your fathers’ works, because they killed them and you do the building [of their tombs].
translations.LAMSA: Therefore you are witnesses, and you approve the works of your fathers; for they killed them, and yet you build their tombs.

anchor.BSB: So you are witnesses consenting to the deeds of your fathers: They killed the prophets, and you build their tombs.

LUK_11_49.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_49.normalized: because of this the wisdom of god said i will send them prophets and apostles some of them they will kill and others they will persecute

translations.NRSVUE: For this reason the Wisdom of God said, ‘I will send them prophets and apostles, some of whom they will kill and persecute,’
translations.DBH: 49 Thus also the Wisdom of God said, ‘I will send prophets and apostles to them, and some of them they will kill and persecute,’
translations.LAMSA: For this reason the wisdom of God also said, Behold, I will send them prophets and apostles, some of whom they will persecute and kill;

anchor.BSB: Because of this, the wisdom of God said, 'I will send them prophets and apostles; some of them they will kill and others they will persecute.

LUK_11_50.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_50.normalized: as a result this generation will be charged with the blood of all the prophets that has been shed since the foundation of the world

translations.NRSVUE: so that this generation may be charged with the blood of all the prophets shed since the foundation of the world,
translations.DBH: 50 so that all the blood of the prophets shed since the foundation of the cosmos will be required of this generation,
translations.LAMSA: That the blood of all the prophets which was shed since the creation of the world may be avenged on this nation;

anchor.BSB: As a result, this generation will be charged with the blood of all the prophets that has been shed since the foundation of the world,

LUK_11_51.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_51.normalized: from the blood of abel to the blood of zechariah who was killed between the altar and the sanctuary yes i tell you all of it will be charged to this generation

translations.NRSVUE: from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zechariah, who perished between the altar and the sanctuary. Yes, I tell you, it will be charged against this generation.
translations.DBH: 51 from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zachariah who perished between the altar and the sanctuary; yes, I tell you, it will be required of this generation.
translations.LAMSA: From the blood of Abel to the blood of Zechariah, who was killed between the temple and the altar; yes, I say to you, it will be avenged on this nation.

anchor.BSB: from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zechariah, who was killed between the altar and the sanctuary. Yes, I tell you, all of it will be charged to this generation.

LUK_11_52.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_52.normalized: woe to you experts in the law for you have taken away the key to knowledge you yourselves have not entered and you have hindered those who were entering

translations.NRSVUE: Woe to you experts in the law! For you have taken away the key of knowledge; you did not enter yourselves, and you hindered those who were entering.”
translations.DBH: 52 Alas for you, the lawyers, because you carried away the key of knowledge; you yourselves did not enter in, and those going in you prevented.”
translations.LAMSA: Woe to you, scribes! for you have taken away the keys of knowledge; you did not enter, and those who were entering you hindered.

anchor.BSB: Woe to you experts in the law! For you have taken away the key to knowledge. You yourselves have not entered, and you have hindered those who were entering.

LUK_12_1.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_1.normalized: beware of the leaven of the pharisees which is hypocrisy

translations.DBH: 1 Meanwhile, as the thousands in the crowd had congregated so that they were treading on one another, he began first to say to his disciples, “Guard yourselves from the yeast of the Pharisees, which is charlatanism.
translations.LAMSA: WHEN a large number of people had gathered together so that they trod on one another, Jesus began to say to his disciples first of all, Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.

anchor.BSB: Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.

LUK_12_2.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_2.normalized: there is nothing concealed that will not be disclosed and nothing hidden that will not be made known

translations.DBH: 2 There is nothing thoroughly veiled that will not be unveiled, or hidden that will not be known.
translations.LAMSA: For there is nothing that is covered that Will not be uncovered; and hidden that will not be known.

anchor.BSB: There is nothing concealed that will not be disclosed, and nothing hidden that will not be made known.

LUK_12_3.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_3.normalized: what you have spoken in the dark will be heard in the daylight and what you have whispered in the inner rooms will be proclaimed from the housetops

translations.DBH: 3 Thus the things you said in darkness will be heard in the light, and what you whisper into an ear in private rooms will be proclaimed on the rooftops.
translations.LAMSA: For whatever you have said in darkness will be heard in the light; and what you have whispered in the ears in the inner chambers will be preached on the housetops.

anchor.BSB: What you have spoken in the dark will be heard in the daylight, and what you have whispered in the inner rooms will be proclaimed from the housetops.

LUK_12_4.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_4.normalized: i tell you my friends do not be afraid of those who kill the body and after that can do no more

translations.NRSVUE: “I tell you, my friends, do not fear those who kill the body and after that can do nothing more.
translations.DBH: 4 And I say to you, my friends, do not be afraid of those who kill the body and thereafter have nothing more they can do.
translations.LAMSA: I say to you, my friends, Do not be afraid of those who kill the body, and after that have nothing more they can do.

anchor.BSB: I tell you, My friends, do not be afraid of those who kill the body and after that can do no more.

LUK_12_5.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_5.normalized: but i will show you whom you should fear fear the one who after you have been killed has authority to throw you into hell yes i tell you fear him

translations.NRSVUE: But I will show you whom to fear: fear the one who, after killing, has authority to cast into hell. Yes, I tell you, fear that one!
translations.DBH: 5 But I shall show you whom you should fear: Fear the one who, after killing, has the power of casting into the Vale of Hinnom. Yes, I tell you, fear this one.
translations.LAMSA: But I will show you of whom to be afraid; of him who after he has killed has the power to throw into hell; yes, I say to you, Fear him.

anchor.BSB: But I will show you whom you should fear: Fear the One who, after you have been killed, has authority to throw you into hell. Yes, I tell you, fear Him!

LUK_12_6.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_6.normalized: are not five sparrows sold for two pennies yet not one of them is forgotten by god

translations.NRSVUE: Are not five sparrows sold for two pennies? Yet not one of them is forgotten in God’s sight.
translations.DBH: 6 Are not five sparrows sold for two pennies? And not one of them is forgotten in God’s sight.
translations.LAMSA: Are not five sparrows sold for two pennies? And yet not one of them is lost before God.

anchor.BSB: Are not five sparrows sold for two pennies? Yet not one of them is forgotten by God.

LUK_12_7.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_7.normalized: and even the very hairs of your head are all numbered so do not be afraid you are worth more than many sparrows

translations.NRSVUE: But even the hairs of your head are all numbered. Do not be afraid; you are of more value than many sparrows.
translations.DBH: 7 Rather, even the hairs of your head have all been numbered. So do not be afraid; you are worth more than many sparrows.
translations.LAMSA: But so far as you are concerned, even the hairs of your head are all numbered; therefore fear not, because you are much more important than many sparrows.

anchor.BSB: And even the very hairs of your head are all numbered. So do not be afraid; you are worth more than many sparrows.

LUK_12_8.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_8.normalized: i tell you everyone who confesses me before men the son of man will also confess him before the angels of god

translations.NRSVUE: “And I tell you, everyone who acknowledges me before others, the Son of Man also will acknowledge before the angels of God,
translations.DBH: 8 But I tell you, everyone who acknowledges me before human beings, the Son of Man will also acknowledge him before the angels of God;
translations.LAMSA: I say to you, Whoever will acknowledge me before men, the Son of man will also acknowledge him before the angels of God.

anchor.BSB: I tell you, everyone who confesses Me before men, the Son of Man will also confess him before the angels of God.

LUK_12_9.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_9.normalized: but whoever denies me before men will be denied before the angels of god

translations.NRSVUE: but whoever denies me before others will be denied before the angels of God.
translations.DBH: 9 and the one who denies me before human beings will be denied before the angels of God.
translations.LAMSA: But he who denies me before men I will deny before the angels of God.

anchor.BSB: But whoever denies Me before men will be denied before the angels of God.

LUK_12_10.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_10.normalized: and everyone who speaks a word against the son of man will be forgiven but whoever blasphemes against the holy spirit will not be forgiven

translations.NRSVUE: And everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.
translations.DBH: 10 And everyone who will speak a word against the Son of Man, it will be excused him; but the one blaspheming against the Holy Spirit will not be excused.
translations.LAMSA: And whoever says a word against the Son of man will be forgiven; but he who blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.

anchor.BSB: And everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.

LUK_12_11.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_11.normalized: when you are brought before synagogues rulers and authorities do not worry about how to defend yourselves or what to say

translations.NRSVUE: When they bring you before the synagogues, the rulers, and the authorities, do not worry about how or what you will answer or what you are to say,
translations.DBH: 11 And when they bring you in before synagogues and rulers and authorities do not be anxious about how or what you may answer in defense or may say;
translations.LAMSA: When they bring you to the synagogues before the leaders and authorities, do not worry how you will answer or what you will say;

anchor.BSB: When you are brought before synagogues, rulers, and authorities, do not worry about how to defend yourselves or what to say.

LUK_12_12.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_12.normalized: for at that time the holy spirit will teach you what you should say

translations.NRSVUE: for the Holy Spirit will teach you at that very hour what you ought to say.”
translations.DBH: 12 for in that hour the Holy Spirit will teach you the things it is necessary to say.”
translations.LAMSA: For the Holy Spirit will teach you at that very hour what you ought to say.

anchor.BSB: For at that time the Holy Spirit will teach you what you should say.

LUK_12_14.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_14.normalized: man who appointed me judge or executor between you

translations.NRSVUE: But he said to him, “Friend, who set me to be a judge or arbitrator over you?”
translations.DBH: 14 But he said to him, “Man, who appointed me judge or arbiter over you?”
translations.LAMSA: Jesus said to him, Man, who appointed me a judge or a property divider over you?

anchor.BSB: Man, who appointed Me judge or executor between you?

LUK_12_15.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_15.normalized: watch out guard yourselves against every form of greed for one s life does not consist in the abundance of his possessions

translations.NRSVUE: And he said to them, “Take care! Be on your guard against all kinds of greed, for one’s life does not consist in the abundance of possessions.”

anchor.BSB: Watch out! Guard yourselves against every form of greed, for one's life does not consist in the abundance of his possessions.

anchor.BSB: And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come back and welcome you into My presence, so that you also may be where I am.

JHN_14_6.speaker: Jesus

translations.LAMSA: And I will ask of my Father, and he will give you another Comforter, to be with you for ever,

JHN_20_15.speaker: Jesus

translations.NRSVUE: Jesus said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping? Whom are you looking for?” Supposing him to be the gardener, she said to him, “Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have laid him, and I will take him away.”
translations.DBH: 15 Jesus says to her, “Madam, why are you weeping? Whom do you seek?” She, thinking that he is the gardener, says to him, “My lord, if you have carried him off, tell me where you put him, and I will take him away.” 15 When, therefore, they had breakfasted, Jesus says to Simon Peter, “Simon son of John, do you love me more than these?”ai He says to him, “Yes, you know that I love you.” He says to him, “Feed my little lambs.”
translations.LAMSA: Jesus said to her, Woman, why do you weep? and whom do you want? She thought he was the gardener, so she said to him, My lord, if you are the one who has taken him away, tell me where you have laid him, and I will go and take him away.

anchor.BSB: Woman, why are you weeping? Whom are you seeking?

translations.NRSVUE: Jesus said to her, “Mary!” She turned and said to him in Hebrew, “Rabbouni!” (which means Teacher).
translations.DBH: 16 Jesus says to her, “Mary.” Turning, she says to him in Hebrew, “Rabbouni” (which means “Teacher”). 16 A second time, he again says to him, “Simon son of John, do you love me?” He says to him, “Yes, Lord, you know that I love you.” He says to him, “Shepherd my flocks.”
translations.LAMSA: Jesus said to her, Mary. She turned around and said to him in Hebrew, Rabbuli! which means, My Teacher!

anchor.BSB: Mary.

REV_2_18.normalized: to the angel of the church in thyatira write these are the words of the son of god whose eyes are like a blazing fire and whose feet are like polished bronze

translations.DBH: 18 “And to the angel of the assembly in Thyatira write: ‘The Son of God, who has eyes like a flame of fire, and his feet like fine brass, says these things:
translations.LAMSA: And to the angel of the church in Thyatira write: These things says the Son of God, who has eyes like a flame of fire, and whose feet are like fine brass from Lebanon;

anchor.BSB: To the angel of the church in Thyatira write: These are the words of the Son of God, whose eyes are like a blazing fire and whose feet are like polished bronze.

REV_2_19.speaker: Jesus
REV_2_19.normalized: i know your deeds your love your faith your service your perseverance and your latest deeds are greater than your first

translations.DBH: 19 “I know your works and love and faith and ministry, as well as your endurance, and your last works are more plentiful than your first.
translations.LAMSA: I know your works and love and faith and service, and also your patience; and your last works are to be more abundant than the first.

anchor.BSB: I know your deeds-your love, your faith, your service, your perseverance-and your latest deeds are greater than your first.

REV_2_20.speaker: Jesus
REV_2_20.normalized: but i have this against you you tolerate that woman jezebel who calls herself a prophetess by her teaching she misleads my servants to be sexually immoral and to eat food sacrificed to idols

translations.DBH: 20 But I hold it against you that you tolerate the woman Jezebel, who calls herself a prophetess, and she teaches and deceitfully persuades my slaves to whore about and to eat sacrifices offered to idols.
translations.LAMSA: Notwithstanding I have a few things against you because you allowed that woman of yours Jezebel, who calls herself a prophetess, to teach and to seduce my servants to commit fornication and to cat things sacrificed to idols.

anchor.BSB: But I have this against you: You tolerate that woman Jezebel, who calls herself a prophetess. By her teaching she misleads My servants to be sexually immoral and to eat food sacrificed to idols.

REV_2_21.speaker: Jesus
REV_2_21.normalized: even though i have given her time to repent of her immorality she is unwilling

translations.DBH: 21 And I gave her time so that she might change her heart, and she does not wish to turn her heart away from her whoring.
translations.LAMSA: And I gave her time to repent, but she did not repent from her fornication.

anchor.BSB: Even though I have given her time to repent of her immorality, she is unwilling.

REV_2_22.speaker: Jesus
REV_2_22.normalized: behold i will cast her onto a bed of sickness and those who commit adultery with her will suffer great tribulation unless they repent of her deeds

translations.DBH: 22 Look: I cast her down onto a sickbed, and those who commit adultery with her into great affliction, should they fail to turn their hearts from her works;
translations.LAMSA: Behold I will cast her into a sick bed and those who commit adultery with her into great tribulation, unless they repent of their deeds.

anchor.BSB: Behold, I will cast her onto a bed of sickness, and those who commit adultery with her will suffer great tribulation unless they repent of her deeds.

REV_2_23.speaker: Jesus
REV_2_23.normalized: then i will strike her children dead and all the churches will know that i am the one who searches minds and hearts and i will repay each of you according to your deeds

translations.DBH: 23 and her children I will strike down with death, and all the assemblies wore such a chaplet specifically as an alternative to the royal diadem of the pre- Republican kings. shall know that I am the one who searches kidneyse and hearts, and I will pay out to you each according to your works.
translations.LAMSA: And I will smite her children with death; and all the churches shall know that I am he who searches the minds and hearts; and I will give to everyone of you according to your works.

anchor.BSB: Then I will strike her children dead, and all the churches will know that I am the One who searches minds and hearts, and I will repay each of you according to your deeds.

REV_2_24.speaker: Jesus
REV_2_24.normalized: but i say to the rest of you in thyatira who do not hold to her teaching and have not learned the so called deep things of satan i will place no further burden upon you

translations.DBH: 24 But I say to you, [and to] the rest of those in Thyatira, however many do not hold to this teach- ing—who have not plumbed the Accuser’s depths, as they say—I am not heaping another burden upon you;
translations.LAMSA: But I say to you, the rest of you in Thyatira, those who do not have this doctrine, and those who have not known, as they say, the depths of Satan, that I will not put upon you another burden.

anchor.BSB: But I say to the rest of you in Thyatira, who do not hold to her teaching and have not learned the so-called deep things of Satan: I will place no further burden upon you

REV_2_25.speaker: Jesus
REV_2_25.normalized: than to hold fast to what you have until i come

translations.DBH: 25 nevertheless, hold onto what you have until I shall come.
translations.LAMSA: But hold fast to that which you already have till I come.

anchor.BSB: than to hold fast to what you have until I come.

REV_2_26.speaker: Jesus
REV_2_26.normalized: and to the one who overcomes and continues in my work until the end i will give authority over the nations

translations.DBH: 26 And the one who conquers, and who abides by my works until the end, to him I will give power over the gentiles—
translations.LAMSA: And he who overcomes and keeps my works until the end, to him I will give authority over the nations;

anchor.BSB: And to the one who overcomes and continues in My work until the end, I will give authority over the nations.

REV_2_27.speaker: Jesus
REV_2_27.normalized: he will rule them with an iron scepter and shatter them like pottery just as i have received authority from my father

translations.DBH: 27 ‘And he will shepherd them with a rod of iron: they are shattered like clay vessels’—28just as I also received it from my Father; and I will give him the Morning Star.
translations.LAMSA: And he shall shepherd them with a rod of iron; like the vessels of the potter, they shall be shattered, even as I was disciplined by my Father.

anchor.BSB: He will rule them with an iron scepter and shatter them like pottery-just as I have received authority from My Father.

REV_2_28.speaker: Jesus
REV_2_28.normalized: and i will give him the morning star

translations.LAMSA: And I will give him the morning star.

anchor.BSB: And I will give him the morning star.

REV_2_29.speaker: Jesus
REV_2_29.normalized: he who has an ear let him hear what the spirit says to the churches

translations.DBH: 29 Whoever has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies.”’
translations.LAMSA: He who has ears, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches.

anchor.BSB: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches.

REV_3_1.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_1.normalized: to the angel of the church in sardis write these are the words of the one who holds the seven spirits of god and the seven stars i know your deeds you have a reputation for being alive yet you are dead

translations.DBH: 1 “And to the angel of the assembly in Sardis write: ‘The one who has the seven Spirits of God and the seven stars says these things: “I know your works—that you have won a name for being alive, yet you are dead.
translations.LAMSA: AND to the angel of the church in Sardis write: These things says he who has the seven Spirits of God and the seven stars: I know your works; you have a name that you are alive and yet you are dead.

anchor.BSB: To the angel of the church in Sardis write: These are the words of the One who holds the seven spirits of God and the seven stars. I know your deeds; you have a reputation for being alive, yet you are dead.

REV_3_2.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_2.normalized: wake up and strengthen what remains which is about to die for i have found your deeds incomplete in the sight of my god

translations.DBH: 2 Be vigilant, fortify the things that remain that are moribund, for I have not found your works to be fulfilled in the eyes of my God;
translations.LAMSA: Awake, and hold fast to the things which remain but are ready to die; for I have not found your works perfect before my God.

anchor.BSB: Wake up and strengthen what remains, which is about to die; for I have found your deeds incomplete in the sight of My God.

REV_3_3.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_3.normalized: remember then what you have received and heard keep it and repent if you do not wake up i will come like a thief and you will not know the hour when i will come upon you

translations.DBH: 3 remember, therefore, how you have received and listened, and keep watch and change your hearts. Should you not be vigilant, then, I shall come like a thief, and you surely would not know at what hour I shall come upon you.
translations.LAMSA: Remember, therefore, just as you have received and heard, so hold fast and repent. And if, therefore, you do not awake, I will come against you as a thief, and you shall not know at what hour I will come upon you.

anchor.BSB: Remember, then, what you have received and heard. Keep it and repent. If you do not wake up, I will come like a thief, and you will not know the hour when I will come upon you.

REV_3_4.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_4.normalized: but you do have a few people in sardis who have not soiled their garments and because they are worthy they will walk with me in white

translations.DBH: 4 But you have in Sardis a few who can be named who did not sully their garments, and they shall walk with me in whites, because they are worthy.
translations.LAMSA: But you have a few members at Sardis who have not defiled their names; and they shall walk with me in white, for they are worthy.

anchor.BSB: But you do have a few people in Sardis who have not soiled their garments, and because they are worthy, they will walk with Me in white.

REV_3_5.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_5.normalized: like them he who overcomes will be dressed in white and i will never blot out his name from the book of life but i will confess his name before my father and his angels

translations.DBH: 5 The one who conquers shall thus be wrapped in white garments, and I most certainly will not expunge his name from the book of life, and I will acknowledge his name before my Father and before his angels.
translations.LAMSA: He who overcomes, the same shall be clothed in white robes; and I will not blot his name out of the book of life, but I will confess his name before my Father and before his angels.

anchor.BSB: Like them, he who overcomes will be dressed in white. And I will never blot out his name from the Book of Life, but I will confess his name before My Father and His angels.

REV_3_6.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_6.normalized: he who has an ear let him hear what the spirit says to the churches

translations.DBH: 6 Whoever has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies.”’
translations.LAMSA: He who has ears, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches.

anchor.BSB: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches.

REV_3_7.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_7.normalized: to the angel of the church in philadelphia write these are the words of the one who is holy and true who holds the key of david what he opens no one can shut and what he shuts no one can open

translations.DBH: 7 “And to the angel of the assembly in Philadelphia write: ‘The holy one, the true one, who holds the key of David, the one who opens—and none shall close—and who closes—and none opens—says these things:
translations.LAMSA: And to the angel of the church in Philadelphia write: These things says he who is the holy one, he who is true, he who has the key of David, he who opens and no man shuts, and shuts, and no man opens;

anchor.BSB: To the angel of the church in Philadelphia write: These are the words of the One who is holy and true, who holds the key of David. What He opens no one can shut, and what He shuts no one can open.

REV_3_8.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_8.normalized: i know your deeds behold i have placed before you an open door which no one can shut i know that you have only a little strength yet you have kept my word and have not denied my name

translations.DBH: 8 “I know your works. See: I have provided an opened door before you, one that none can close, for you have small power, yet you have kept my word and not denied my name.
translations.LAMSA: I know your works and behold, I have set before you an open door which no man can lock, for you have but little strength and yet you have obeyed my word and have not denied my name.

anchor.BSB: I know your deeds. Behold, I have placed before you an open door, which no one can shut. I know that you have only a little strength, yet you have kept My word and have not denied My name.

REV_3_9.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_9.normalized: as for those who belong to the synagogue of satan who claim to be jews but are liars instead i will make them come and bow down at your feet and they will know that i love you

translations.DBH: 9 See: I make a gift of some from the synagogue of the Accuser—those who say they are Judaeans, yet are not, but are lying. Look: I will make it so that they shall come and prostrate themselves before your feet, and they shall know that I have loved you.
translations.LAMSA: Behold, I turn over those of the synagogue of Satan, who say that they are Jews and are not, but do lie; behold, I will make them to come and worship before your feet, and to know that I have loved you.

anchor.BSB: As for those who belong to the synagogue of Satan, who claim to be Jews but are liars instead, I will make them come and bow down at your feet, and they will know that I love you.

REV_3_10.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_10.normalized: because you have kept my command to persevere i will also keep you from the hour of testing that is about to come upon the whole world to test those who dwell on the earth

translations.DBH: 10 Because you have kept my instruction to endure, I will also keep you from the hour of the trial that is about to come upon the whole inhabited world, to test those who dwell upon the earth.
translations.LAMSA: Because you have kept the word of my patience, I also will keep you from the hour of temptation which shall come upon all the world to try those who dwell upon earth.

anchor.BSB: Because you have kept My command to persevere, I will also keep you from the hour of testing that is about to come upon the whole world, to test those who dwell on the earth.

REV_3_11.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_11.normalized: i am coming soon hold fast to what you have so that no one will take your crown

translations.DBH: 11 I am coming very quickly; hold fast to what you have so that no one take your chaplet.
translations.LAMSA: Behold, I come quickly. Hold that fast which you have, so that no man take your crown.

anchor.BSB: I am coming soon. Hold fast to what you have, so that no one will take your crown.

REV_3_12.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_12.normalized: the one who overcomes i will make a pillar in the temple of my god and he will never again leave it upon him i will write the name of my god and the name of the city of my god the new jerusalem that comes down out of heaven from my god and my new name

translations.DBH: 12 The one who conquers, I will make him a pillar in the sanctuary of my God, and he shall surely not go out of it any longer, and I will inscribe upon him my God’s name and the name of my God’s city—the New Jerusalem descending out of heaven from my God—as well as my new name.
translations.LAMSA: He who overcomes I will make a pillar in the temple of my God, and he shall not go out again; and I will write upon him the name of my God and the name of the new Jerusalem which comes down out of heaven from my God; and I will write upon him my new name.

anchor.BSB: The one who overcomes I will make a pillar in the temple of My God, and he will never again leave it. Upon him I will write the name of My God, and the name of the city of My God (the new Jerusalem that comes down out of heaven from My God), and My new name.

REV_3_13.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_13.normalized: he who has an ear let him hear what the spirit says to the churches

translations.DBH: 13 Whoever has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies.”’
translations.LAMSA: He who has ears, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches.

anchor.BSB: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches.

REV_3_14.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_14.normalized: to the angel of the church in laodicea write these are the words of the amen the faithful and true witness the originator of god s creation

translations.DBH: 14 “And to the angel of the assembly in Laodicea write: ‘The Amen, the witness faithful and true, the origing of God’s creation, says these things:
translations.LAMSA: And to the angel of the church in Laodicea write: These things says the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the beginning of the creation of God;

anchor.BSB: To the angel of the church in Laodicea write: These are the words of the Amen, the faithful and true Witness, the Originator of God's creation.

REV_3_15.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_15.normalized: i know your deeds you are neither cold nor hot how i wish you were one or the other

translations.DBH: 15 “I know your works—that you are neither cold nor hot. I would that you were cold or hot.
translations.LAMSA: I know your works, that you are neither cold nor hot; it is better to be either cold or hot.

anchor.BSB: I know your deeds; you are neither cold nor hot. How I wish you were one or the other!

REV_3_16.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_16.normalized: so because you are lukewarm neither hot nor cold i am about to vomit you out of my mouth

translations.DBH: 16 Thus, since you are lukewarm, and neither hot nor cold, I am about to vomit you from my mouth.
translations.LAMSA: So then because you are lukewarm, and neither cold nor hot, I will spue you out of my mouth.

anchor.BSB: So because you are lukewarm-neither hot nor cold-I am about to vomit you out of My mouth!

REV_3_17.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_17.normalized: you say i am rich i have grown wealthy and need nothing but you do not realize that you are wretched pitiful poor blind and naked

translations.DBH: 17 For you say, ‘I am rich, and have been enriched, and need nothing,’ and you do not realize that you are someone wretched and pitiable and destitute and blind and naked.
translations.LAMSA: You say, I am rich and my wealth has increased and I need nothing; and you do not know that you are miserable and a wanderer and poor and blind and naked.

anchor.BSB: You say, 'I am rich; I have grown wealthy and need nothing.' But you do not realize that you are wretched, pitiful, poor, blind, and naked.

REV_3_18.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_18.normalized: i counsel you to buy from me gold refined by fire so that you may become rich white garments so that you may be clothed and your shameful nakedness not exposed and salve to anoint your eyes so that you may see

translations.DBH: 18 I counsel you to buy gold refined by fire from me so that you may be rich, and white garments so that you may be clad and the shame of your nakedness may not be made manifest, and salve to anoint your eyes so that you may see.
translations.LAMSA: I advise you to buy of me gold refined in the fire, that you may become rich; and white raiment, that you may be clothed, so that the shame of your nakedness may not be seen; and anoint your eyes with salve, that you may see.

anchor.BSB: I counsel you to buy from Me gold refined by fire so that you may become rich, white garments so that you may be clothed and your shameful nakedness not exposed, and salve to anoint your eyes so that you may see.

REV_3_19.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_19.normalized: those i love i rebuke and discipline therefore be earnest and repent

translations.DBH: 19 As many as I love I rebuke and discipline; so be zealous and change your heart.
translations.LAMSA: I rebuke and chastise all those whom I love; be zealous, therefore, and repent.

anchor.BSB: Those I love I rebuke and discipline. Therefore be earnest and repent.

REV_3_20.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_20.normalized: behold i stand at the door and knock if anyone hears my voice and opens the door i will come in and dine with him and he with me

translations.DBH: 20 Look: I stand at the door and knock; should anyone hear my voice and open the door, I will enter in to him, and I will dine with him and he with me.
translations.LAMSA: Behold, I stand at the door and knock; if any man hear my voice and open the door, I will come in to him and will sup with him, and he with me.

anchor.BSB: Behold, I stand at the door and knock. If anyone hears My voice and opens the door, I will come in and dine with him, and he with Me.

REV_3_21.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_21.normalized: to the one who overcomes i will grant the right to sit with me on my throne just as i overcame and sat down with my father on his throne

translations.DBH: 21 The one who conquers, I will grant it to him to sit with me upon my throne, just as I also conquered and sat with my Father on his throne.
translations.LAMSA: To him who overcomes I will grant to sit with me on my throne, even as I also overcame and have sat down with my Father on his throne.

anchor.BSB: To the one who overcomes, I will grant the right to sit with Me on My throne, just as I overcame and sat down with My Father on His throne.

REV_3_22.speaker: Jesus
REV_3_22.normalized: he who has an ear let him hear what the spirit says to the churches

translations.DBH: 22 Whoever has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies.”’”
translations.LAMSA: He who has ears, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches.

anchor.BSB: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches.

REV_4_1.speaker: Jesus
REV_4_1.normalized: come up here and i will show you what must happen after these things

anchor.BSB: Come up here, and I will show you what must happen after these things.

REV_16_15.speaker: Jesus
REV_16_15.normalized: behold i am coming like a thief blessed is the one who remains awake and clothed so that he will not go naked and let his shame be exposed

translations.DBH: 15 “Look: I am coming like a thief; how blissful the one who remains vigilant and keeps his clothes on, lest he walk naked and they see his shame.”
translations.LAMSA: And behold, I come as a thief. Blessed is he who watches and keeps his garments, lest he must walk naked and they see his shame.

anchor.BSB: Behold, I am coming like a thief. Blessed is the one who remains awake and clothed, so that he will not go naked and let his shame be exposed.

REV_22_7.speaker: Jesus
REV_22_7.normalized: behold i am coming soon blessed is the one who keeps the words of prophecy in this book

translations.DBH: 7 “And look: I am coming quickly. How blissful the one who keeps the words of the prophecy of this book.”
translations.LAMSA: Behold, I am coming soon; blessed is he who keeps the sayings of the prophecy of this book.

anchor.BSB: Behold, I am coming soon. Blessed is the one who keeps the words of prophecy in this book.

REV_22_12.speaker: Jesus
REV_22_12.normalized: behold i am coming soon and my reward is with me to give to each one according to what he has done

translations.DBH: 12 “Look: I am coming quickly, and with me is the recompense I have to render to each, according to what his work is.ba
translations.LAMSA: Behold, I am coming soon and my reward is with me, to give every man according as his work shall be.

anchor.BSB: Behold, I am coming soon, and My reward is with Me, to give to each one according to what he has done.

REV_22_13.speaker: Jesus
REV_22_13.normalized: i am the alpha and the omega the first and the last the beginning and the end

translations.DBH: 13 I am the alpha and the omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.”
translations.LAMSA: I am Aleph and Tau, the beginning and the end, the first and the last.

anchor.BSB: I am the Alpha and the Omega, the First and the Last, the Beginning and the End.

REV_22_16.speaker: Jesus
REV_22_16.normalized: i jesus have sent my angel to give you this testimony for the churches i am the root and the offspring of david the bright morning star

translations.DBH: 16 “I Jesus sent my angel to attest these things to you for the assemblies. I am the root and the offspring of David, the bright Morning Star.”bc
translations.LAMSA: I, Jesus, have sent my angel to testify to you these things in the churches. I am the root and the offspring of David, the bright morning star.

anchor.BSB: I, Jesus, have sent My angel to give you this testimony for the churches. I am the Root and the Offspring of David, the bright Morning Star.

translations.DBH: 20 The one who attests these things says, “Yes, I am coming quickly.” Amen, [yes,] come, Lord Jesus.
translations.LAMSA: He who testifies these things says, Surely I am coming soon. Amen. Come, Lord Jesus.

anchor.BSB: Yes, I am coming soon.
